title: Past Simple - Irregular Verbs (Top 30)
description: Learn the 30 most common irregular verbs in past simple through a travel blog about Istanbul.
descriptionTr: İstanbul hakkında bir seyahat blogu üzerinden en yaygın 30 düzensiz fiilin geçmiş zamanını öğren.

vocabulary.example: I went to Istanbul last summer.
vocabulary.extra: We went to the Grand Bazaar on Saturday.

vocabulary.example: I saw the Blue Mosque for the first time.
vocabulary.extra: We saw beautiful sunsets from the Bosphorus.

vocabulary.example: I ate the best kebab in Sultanahmet.
vocabulary.extra: We ate fresh fish at the Galata Bridge.

vocabulary.example: I drank Turkish tea everywhere I went.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gittiğim her yerde Türk çayı içtim.
vocabulary.extra: She drank fresh pomegranate juice at the bazaar.

vocabulary.example: I bought a beautiful carpet in Istanbul.
vocabulary.exampleTr: İstanbul'da güzel bir halı satın aldım.
vocabulary.extra: She bought Turkish delight for her family.

vocabulary.example: I took many photos of the Hagia Sophia.
vocabulary.extra: We took a boat tour on the Bosphorus.

vocabulary.example: I made new friends during my trip.
vocabulary.extra: She made a reservation at the hotel.

vocabulary.example: My friend Jake came to Istanbul with me.
vocabulary.extra: Many tourists came to Turkey last year.
vocabulary.extraTr: Geçen yıl birçok turist Türkiye'ye geldi.

vocabulary.example: We had an amazing time in Istanbul.
vocabulary.extra: I had breakfast at the hotel every morning.

vocabulary.example: I got a taxi from the airport.
vocabulary.extra: We got lost in the old streets of Balat.

vocabulary.example: I knew nothing about Istanbul before my trip.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Seyahatimden önce İstanbul hakkında hiçbir şey bilmiyordum.
vocabulary.extra: She knew the best restaurants in the city.

vocabulary.example: I thought the food was incredible.
vocabulary.extra: We thought the bazaar would be small, but it was huge!
vocabulary.extraTr: Çarşının küçük olacağını düşündük ama devasa çıktı!

vocabulary.example: I found a great little café near Taksim.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Taksim yakınında harika küçük bir kafe buldum.
vocabulary.extra: We found cheap souvenirs at the Spice Bazaar.

vocabulary.example: The waiter gave us free tea after dinner.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Garson bize yemekten sonra bedava çay verdi.
vocabulary.extra: I gave my friend a Turkish coffee set as a gift.
vocabulary.extraTr: Arkadaşıma hediye olarak Türk kahvesi seti verdim.

vocabulary.example: I told Jake about the best places to visit.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Jake'e gezilecek en iyi yerleri anlattım.
vocabulary.extra: The tour guide told us the history of Topkapı Palace.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tur rehberi bize Topkapı Sarayı'nın tarihini anlattı.

vocabulary.example: We left Istanbul on Sunday morning.
vocabulary.extra: I left my heart in Istanbul!

vocabulary.example: I felt so happy when I visited the Bosphorus.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Boğaz'ı ziyaret ettiğimde çok mutlu hissettim.
vocabulary.extra: We felt tired after walking all day.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bütün gün yürüdükten sonra yorgun hissettik.

vocabulary.example: Istanbul became my favorite city.
vocabulary.extra: The weather became cold in the evening.

vocabulary.example: We ran to catch the ferry.
vocabulary.extra: I ran along the Bosphorus every morning.

vocabulary.example: I wrote a blog post about my Istanbul trip.
vocabulary.exampleTr: İstanbul seyahatim hakkında bir blog yazısı yazdım.
vocabulary.extra: She wrote postcards to her friends back home.

vocabulary.example: I read about Istanbul's history before my trip.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Seyahatimden önce İstanbul'un tarihi hakkında okudum.
vocabulary.extra: She read the menu in Turkish — very impressive!

vocabulary.example: I spoke a little Turkish at the market.
vocabulary.extra: The hotel manager spoke perfect English.

vocabulary.example: I met many kind people in Istanbul.
vocabulary.extra: We met our tour guide at the hotel lobby.

vocabulary.example: I paid 50 lira for the boat tour.
vocabulary.extra: We paid with credit card at the restaurant.

vocabulary.example: The man sold handmade jewelry at the bazaar.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Adam çarşıda el yapımı takı sattı.
vocabulary.extra: They sold out all the baklava before noon.

vocabulary.example: I sent photos to my family every day.
vocabulary.extra: She sent a postcard from Istanbul.

vocabulary.example: We caught the last ferry to Kadıköy.
vocabulary.extra: I almost didn't catch my flight home.

vocabulary.example: I flew from Ankara to Istanbul.
vocabulary.extra: We flew Turkish Airlines — great service!
vocabulary.extraTr: Türk Hava Yolları ile uçtuk — harika hizmet!

vocabulary.example: We drove along the coast to the Black Sea.
vocabulary.extra: He drove us to the airport early in the morning.

vocabulary.example: I lost my map in the Grand Bazaar.
vocabulary.extra: She lost her phone on the bus, but someone returned it.
vocabulary.extraTr: Otobüste telefonunu kaybetti ama biri geri getirdi.

grammar.title: Past Simple — Irregular Verbs + Was/Were
grammar.explanation: Some verbs don't add -ed in the past. They change completely! You must memorize each one. The verb 'be' becomes 'was' (I/he/she/it) or 'were' (you/we/they). Questions and negatives still use 'did/didn't' + base form — just like regular verbs.
grammar.explanationTr: Bazı fiiller geçmiş zamanda -ed almaz. Tamamen değişirler! Her birini ezberlemen gerekir. 'Be' fiili 'was' (I/he/she/it) veya 'were' (you/we/they) olur. Sorular ve olumsuz cümleler hala 'did/didn't' + yalın fiil kullanır — tıpkı düzenli fiiller gibi.
table: I ate fish. She bought a bag.
I didn't go / I didn't see
I didn't eat meat. She didn't buy it.
Did you go? / Did she see?
Did you eat? Did she buy a carpet?
I was / He was / She was
Was → I, he, she, it ile kullanılır
I was tired. She was happy.
You were / We were / They were
Were → you, we, they ile kullanılır
You were great. We were at the hotel.
Was he at home? Were they happy?
He wasn't late. They weren't bored.

tips.en: go → went, come → came, become → became
tips.tr: Bu üçlü benzer değişim gösterir — hepsinin sonu '-ame/-ent' gibi

tips.en: think → thought, buy → bought, catch → caught
tips.tr: 'ought' grubu: düşünmek, satın almak, yakalamak — hepsi '-ought/-aught' olur

tips.en: feel → felt, leave → left, meet → met
tips.tr: Kısa değişim grubu: sadece son harfler değişir

tips.en: Did you go? (NOT: Did you went?)
tips.tr: Soru ve olumsuzda ASLA V2 kullanma! Did + V1 (yalın fiil)
'read' yazılışı aynı kalır ama okunuşu değişir: 'riid' → 'red'

tips.en: I was happy. NOT: I were happy.

reading.text:
Last summer, I went to Istanbul for five days. It was my first time there, and I had an amazing experience!

On the first day, I took a taxi from the airport to my hotel in Sultanahmet. I saw the Blue Mosque from the car window — it was beautiful! I felt so excited.

The next morning, I ate a traditional Turkish breakfast at a small café. I drank three cups of tea! Then I went to the Grand Bazaar and bought souvenirs for my family. I found beautiful handmade ceramics and paid only 80 lira.

On day three, I met a local guide named Ahmet. He told me many stories about Istanbul's history. We went to Topkapı Palace and I saw where the sultans lived. I took over 200 photos!

I also flew to the Asian side by ferry — well, I didn't fly, I took the ferry! I ate fresh fish at Kadıköy market and drank fresh pomegranate juice. The food was incredible.

On my last day, I wrote postcards and sent them to my friends. I left Istanbul with a heavy heart. I thought about my trip all the way home. Istanbul became my favorite city in the world. I lost my heart there, and I knew I would come back!
reading.textTr:
Geçen yaz beş günlüğüne İstanbul'a gittim. İlk kez gidiyordum ve harika bir deneyim yaşadım!

İlk gün havalimanından Sultanahmet'teki otelime taksi tuttum. Araba camından Sultanahmet Camii'ni gördüm — çok güzeldi! Çok heyecanlı hissettim.

Ertesi sabah küçük bir kafede geleneksel Türk kahvaltısı yaptım. Üç fincan çay içtim! Sonra Kapalıçarşı'ya gittim ve aileme hediyelik eşyalar aldım. Güzel el yapımı seramikler buldum ve sadece 80 lira ödedim.

Üçüncü gün Ahmet adında yerel bir rehberle tanıştım. Bana İstanbul'un tarihi hakkında birçok hikaye anlattı. Topkapı Sarayı'na gittik ve sultanların yaşadığı yeri gördüm. 200'den fazla fotoğraf çektim!

Ayrıca Anadolu yakasına vapurla geçtim — yani uçmadım, vapur aldım! Kadıköy pazarında taze balık yedim ve taze nar suyu içtim. Yemekler inanılmazdı.

Son günümde kartpostal yazdım ve arkadaşlarıma gönderdim. İstanbul'dan ağır bir kalple ayrıldım. Eve dönüş boyunca seyahatimi düşündüm. İstanbul dünyadaki en sevdiğim şehir oldu. Kalbimi orada kaybettim ve geri döneceğimi biliyordum!

questions.question: How many days did the writer spend in Istanbul?
What did the writer buy at the Grand Bazaar?
Who told the writer about Istanbul's history?
What did the writer eat at Kadıköy market?
How did the writer feel about leaving Istanbul?
options: Sad — left with a heavy heart

dialog.title: What Did You Do in Istanbul?
dialog.context: Eyüp, Amerikalı arkadaşı Jake ile video arama yapıyor. Jake, Eyüp'ün İstanbul seyahatini merak ediyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hey Eyüp! So, how was your Istanbul trip?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: It was amazing, Jake! I had the best time.
lines.textTr: Harikaydı, Jake! En güzel zamanı geçirdim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Awesome! What did you do on the first day?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I went to the Blue Mosque and I saw the Hagia Sophia. They were incredible!
lines.textTr: Sultanahmet Camii'ne gittim ve Ayasofya'yı gördüm. İnanılmazdılar!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Did you take a lot of photos?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes! I took over 500 photos. My phone ran out of storage!
lines.textTr: Evet! 500'den fazla fotoğraf çektim. Telefonumun hafızası doldu!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Ha! What about the food? Did you eat anything special?
lines.textTr: Ha! Peki yemekler? Özel bir şey yedin mi?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Oh man, I ate so much! I had kebab, fresh fish, and baklava. I drank Turkish tea everywhere.
lines.textTr: Aman tanrım, çok yedim! Kebap, taze balık ve baklava yedim. Her yerde Türk çayı içtim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That sounds delicious. Did you buy anything at the bazaar?
lines.textTr: Çok lezzetli görünüyor. Çarşıda bir şey aldın mı?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I bought a carpet and some spices. I also found beautiful ceramics and I sent some to my mom.
lines.textTr: Bir halı ve biraz baharat aldım. Ayrıca güzel seramikler buldum ve anneme gönderdim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Nice! Did you meet any interesting people?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes, I met a tour guide named Ahmet. He told me amazing stories about the city.
lines.textTr: Evet, Ahmet adında bir tur rehberiyle tanıştım. Bana şehir hakkında harika hikayeler anlattı.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Did you go to the Asian side too?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yes! I took the ferry to Kadıköy. I felt like a real İstanbullu on that boat.
lines.textTr: Evet! Kadıköy'e vapurla gittim. O vapurda kendimi gerçek bir İstanbullu gibi hissettim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: When did you come back home?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: I flew back on Sunday. I left Istanbul with a heavy heart. I wrote a blog post about it!
lines.textTr: Pazar günü uçtum. İstanbul'dan ağır kalple ayrıldım. Hakkında bir blog yazısı yazdım!

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I read your blog! It was great. Now I want to go to Istanbul too!
lines.textTr: Blogunu okudum! Harikaydı. Şimdi ben de İstanbul'a gitmek istiyorum!

questions.question: How many photos did Eyüp take?
What did Eyüp buy at the bazaar?
How did Eyüp go to Kadıköy?
What did Eyüp do after his trip?

fillBlanks.sentence: She ___ beautiful photos of the mosque. (take)
We ___ fresh fish at the market. (eat)
I ___ a carpet at the Grand Bazaar. (buy)
They ___ Turkish tea at every café. (drink)
We ___ many kind people on the trip. (meet)
I ___ a blog post about my trip. (write)
We ___ the last ferry to Kadıköy. (catch)
I ___ my phone on the bus. (lose)

multipleChoice.question: 'go' fiilinin geçmiş zaman hali (past simple) nedir?
"I ___ a great time in Istanbul." Boşluğa ne gelir?
"Did you ___ any souvenirs?" Boşluğa ne gelir?
"___ you see the Blue Mosque?" Boşluğa ne gelir?

sentenceOrder.correct: I went to Istanbul last summer
She bought a carpet at the bazaar
We took the ferry to Kadıköy
He told me about the history
They flew from Ankara to Istanbul
I wrote a blog post about my trip

translation.english: I went to Istanbul last summer.
acceptAlso: I went to Istanbul last summer
Last summer I went to Istanbul

translation.turkish: Çarşıda güzel bir halı satın aldım.
translation.english: I bought a beautiful carpet at the bazaar.
acceptAlso: I bought a beautiful carpet at the bazaar
I bought a nice carpet at the bazaar

translation.english: We took the ferry to Kadıköy.
acceptAlso: We went to Kadıköy by ferry
We took the ferry to Kadikoy

translation.turkish: Seyahatim hakkında bir blog yazısı yazdım.
translation.english: I wrote a blog post about my trip.
acceptAlso: I wrote a blog post about my trip
